description_for_human: Free-API SOC enrichment (CISA KEV, FIRST EPSS, MITRE ATT&CK, abuse.ch, GreyNoise, AbuseIPDB, crt.sh, ransomware.live, OTX, HIBP, OSV.dev, CIRCL hashlookup, MITRE D3FEND) for Security Copilot.
description_for_model:
Community SOC pack that enriches security investigations using free, no-payment APIs. Call these skills whenever a prompt mentions a CVE, IP, domain, URL, file hash, ransomware group or victim organisation.

Skill selection hints:
- CVE exploitation status: kev_lookup, kev_search (CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog).
- CVE exploitation probability: epss_score (FIRST EPSS).
- MITRE ATT&CK technique lookup or search: attack_technique, attack_search.
- Malware sample, hash or URL analysis: malwarebazaar_lookup / malwarebazaar_recent, urlhaus_lookup_url, urlhaus_lookup_host.
- IOC enrichment for IPs, domains, URLs or hashes tied to active campaigns: threatfox_recent, threatfox_search.
- IP reputation and scanner-noise classification: greynoise_classify, abuseipdb_check.
- Subdomain enumeration and attack-surface discovery for a domain: crtsh_subdomains (uses Certificate Transparency logs).
- Ransomware leak-site activity (victims, groups, country-level impact): ransomware_live_recent, ransomware_live_by_group, ransomware_live_by_country, ransomware_live_groups.
- Open-source software vulnerabilities by package + version, git commit, or vulnerability id (OSV / GHSA / CVE): osv_query_package, osv_query_commit, osv_get_vuln (OSV.dev).
- NSRL known-good file lookup by MD5 / SHA1 / SHA256: circl_hashlookup_md5, circl_hashlookup_sha1, circl_hashlookup_sha256 (CIRCL hashlookup). known=false is the SOC-actionable signal.
- Defensive-technique mappings against MITRE ATT&CK (D3FEND): d3fend_defenses_for_attack, d3fend_attacks_for_defense.

Never invent data the tools did not return. Cite the source endpoint when presenting results (for example: "per CISA KEV", "per ransomware.live").
